parts.text: My dearest son, another week has passed, and still, no word. My heart aches with a silence that is louder than any cannon fire. I replay your last letter in my mind, searching for signs, for reassurance that you are well. Is the mail delayed? Are you too busy to write, or perhaps... no, I mustn't let my thoughts wander there. I pray every night, my precious boy, for your safety, for a moment of peace for you. Just a scribble, a single word, would be enough to calm this mother's racing heart. Please, if you can, send something soon. Your silence is a heavy burden, my love. All my love, Your worried Mother.

My precious boy, still no letter. The emptiness of the mailbox mirrors the ache in my chest. Each day without word feels like an eternity, and my imagination conjures a thousand anxieties. I try to busy myself, to focus on the chores, but my thoughts drift constantly to you. Are you cold? Are you hungry? Are you safe? I cling to the hope that no news is good news, a foolish whisper perhaps, but it's all I have. Please, my heart, send a word, a sign, something to ease this crushing worry. Your mother, who misses you more than words can say.

My dearest, my heart! Your words, though few, still carry the weight of your presence, and for that, I am eternally grateful. 'Doesn't reply back,' you wrote... are you speaking of someone else, my love? Or are you, perhaps, frustrated by the slowness of our letters reaching each other? My mind races with possibilities, mostly, I confess, with worry for you. I pray that you are safe, that this war has not dulled your spirit or left you too weary to express yourself. Please, my son, tell me more when you can. Know that I am always here, waiting, praying, and sending every ounce of my love to you. Your ever-loving, ever-anxious Mother.

My dearest, your words, so few yet so heavy, 'doesn't reply back.' My heart contracts with a fear I cannot name, a thousand scenarios rushing through my mind. Who doesn't reply, my love? Is it me? Or are you speaking of someone else, a friend, a comrade? The brevity of your message makes me desperately anxious, my son. Are you hurt? Are you in a place where you cannot write more clearly? I beg of you, my precious boy, send me a definite sign that you are well, that you are whole. This uncertainty is a torment. Write to me, clearly, immediately. Your frantic Mother.

Dear Mom,
hello mom, i miss you.

My dearest son, oh, what a joy it was to see your familiar greeting and to know you miss me! My heart swelled with such relief, you cannot imagine. I miss you too, my precious boy, more than words can ever convey. Your letter is a beacon of light in these dark times. I pray it finds you well and safe, and that you are keeping your spirits up. We are all well here, and the garden is just beginning to bloom, a small comfort. Write to me again soon, my love, even just a few words. They mean the world to your lonely, loving Mother.

Dear Mom,
hello mom, i miss you.

My dearest son, your words 'hello mom, I miss you' were a balm to my weary soul, a whisper of spring after a long winter! To know you are thinking of me, that you miss your old mother, fills my heart with a warmth that chases away the cold fear. I miss you fiercely, my precious boy. I pray this letter finds you in good health and spirits, and that you are not wanting for anything. We are well here, the sun is shining, and I find myself humming old tunes, just thinking of you. Write again soon, my love, and know that you are always in my prayers and my heart. All my love, Your Mother.

Dear Mom,
i dont feel so good

My dearest boy, your words, 'I don't feel so good,' have pierced my heart with a mother's deepest fear. Oh, my precious son, what troubles you? Is it a chill, a wound, or is your spirit simply weary from the endless hardship? My mind races, picturing you unwell, far from my comforting touch. I pray it is nothing serious, my love, and that you are receiving all the care you need. Please, if you can, write more, even just a few more words, to ease this dreadful worry that grips me. Your safety, your health, is all that matters to me. All my love and fervent prayers, Your devoted, anxious Mother.
